terms.definition: Minor surgical removal of part or all of a toenail under local anaesthesia to treat ingrown or recurrent nail pathology.
terms.exampleSentence: Nail surgery was scheduled to address her recurrent ingrown great toenail despite conservative care.
terms.contextNotes: Performed under digital ring block local anaesthesia.

terms.definition: Surgical removal of a longitudinal section of toenail along with its matrix to permanently narrow the nail plate.
terms.exampleSentence: Partial nail avulsion of the medial border was performed with phenolisation of the matrix.
terms.contextNotes: Combined with phenol gives 95%+ success rate for ingrown nails.

terms.definition: Surgical removal of the entire nail plate, typically reserved for severely dystrophic or fungal nails.
terms.exampleSentence: Total nail avulsion was indicated due to severe onychomycosis unresponsive to systemic antifungals.
terms.contextNotes: Without matrixectomy, dystrophic regrowth is common.

terms.definition: Chemical ablation of the nail matrix using 80% phenol applied for 1-3 minutes to prevent regrowth after nail avulsion.
terms.exampleSentence: Phenolisation of the matrix was performed with three 60-second applications of 80% phenol.
terms.contextNotes: Irrigate copiously with saline or alcohol after application.

terms.definition: Sharp removal of hyperkeratotic skin using a scalpel to reduce pressure and reveal underlying pathology.
terms.exampleSentence: Debridement of callus over the first metatarsal head revealed an underlying neuropathic ulcer.
terms.contextNotes: Reduces peak plantar pressure by up to 30%.

terms.definition: Surgical removal of necrotic, sloughy or callused tissue from a wound bed to promote granulation and healing.
terms.exampleSentence: Sharp debridement of the ulcer removed all sloughy tissue, revealing a healthy granulating base.
terms.contextNotes: Contraindicated in critical limb ischaemia or dry gangrene.

terms.definition: Below-knee plaster cast moulded to the foot and leg to evenly redistribute plantar pressure for neuropathic ulcer offloading.
terms.exampleSentence: Total contact casting was applied weekly and the plantar ulcer healed within 6 weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Gold-standard offloading device for neuropathic plantar ulcers.

terms.definition: Removable below-knee pneumatic boot used to offload plantar ulcers when total contact casting is unsuitable.
terms.exampleSentence: An Aircast walker was issued and rendered irremovable to enforce concordance with offloading.
terms.contextNotes: Wrapping with cohesive bandage converts to instant TCC.

terms.definition: Adhesive felt cut to redistribute pressure away from at-risk or ulcerated plantar areas as temporary offloading.
terms.exampleSentence: Felt padding was applied around the ulcer to deflect pressure away from the wound margin.
terms.contextNotes: Replace at each visit; not a substitute for definitive offloading.

terms.definition: Custom or prefabricated in-shoe devices designed to control foot function (functional) or accommodate deformity (accommodative).
terms.exampleSentence: Custom functional orthotics were prescribed to control her excessive midfoot pronation.
terms.contextNotes: Functional = correct mechanics; accommodative = redistribute pressure.

terms.definition: Handheld 5-10 MHz ultrasound device used to detect arterial flow, measure ABPI and characterise waveforms in pedal arteries.
terms.exampleSentence: An 8 MHz Doppler probe was used to assess pedal pulses and calculate the ankle-brachial pressure index.
terms.contextNotes: Use generous coupling gel; angle probe at 45-60 degrees.

terms.definition: Calibrated nylon Semmes-Weinstein filament that buckles at 10g of force, used to test plantar protective sensation.
terms.exampleSentence: The 10g monofilament was applied to ten plantar sites; sensation was absent at four.
terms.contextNotes: Discard after 100 uses; recovery period needed between patients.

terms.definition: Calibrated tuning fork used to assess vibration sensation at the hallux and medial malleolus during neurological screening.
terms.exampleSentence: Vibration was absent at the hallux when tested with a 128Hz tuning fork.
terms.contextNotes: Rydel-Seiffer variant gives quantitative score 0-8.

terms.definition: Sterile single-use blade (commonly No.10 or No.15) mounted on a handle for sharp debridement of callus and ulcers.
terms.exampleSentence: A No.10 scalpel blade was used to debride the hyperkeratotic plantar callus.
terms.contextNotes: Single-use; dispose into sharps bin immediately after use.

terms.definition: Heavy-duty hinged cutting instrument used to trim thickened or onychogryphotic nails too tough for standard clippers.
terms.exampleSentence: Nail nippers were used to reduce the onychogryphotic hallux nail before burring.
terms.contextNotes: Autoclave between patients; check spring action.

terms.definition: Electric rotary instrument fitted with abrasive burrs used to reduce nail thickness and smooth callus.
terms.exampleSentence: A nail drill with a sapphire burr was used to thin the onychauxic toenails.
terms.contextNotes: Use dust extraction to limit fungal spore inhalation.

terms.definition: Structured patient education covering daily inspection, footwear, skin care and red-flag symptoms to prevent ulceration.
terms.exampleSentence: Diabetic foot care education was reinforced, including daily inspection and avoidance of barefoot walking.
terms.contextNotes: Reinforce at every contact; provide written information in plain English.

terms.definition: Patient self-examination of both feet each day, using a mirror if needed, to detect early skin breakdown or injury.
terms.exampleSentence: I encouraged her to perform daily foot inspection using a mirror to check the soles.
terms.contextNotes: Critical in neuropathy where injuries are painless and easily missed.

terms.definition: Documented informed consent covering risks of bleeding, infection, regrowth, scarring and phenol burn before nail procedures.
terms.exampleSentence: Written consent for nail surgery was obtained, including discussion of regrowth and infection risks.
terms.contextNotes: Document risks, benefits, alternatives and patient questions.

terms.definition: Multidisciplinary team review involving podiatry, vascular, diabetes and orthotics to plan management of complex diabetic foot cases.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient was discussed at the weekly MDT diabetic foot round and listed for angioplasty.
terms.contextNotes: NICE recommends MDT review within one working day for active foot disease.

terms.definition: Onward referral for assessment of revascularisation options when peripheral arterial disease threatens limb viability.
terms.exampleSentence: Urgent referral to vascular surgery was made given the non-healing ulcer and ABPI of 0.5.
terms.contextNotes: Include ABPI, Doppler waveforms and wound photos in referral.

terms.definition: Verbal and written instruction on minimising weight-bearing through ulcerated or at-risk foot regions.
terms.exampleSentence: Offloading advice included consistent use of the Aircast walker for all weight-bearing activity.
terms.contextNotes: Concordance is the single biggest predictor of healing.

terms.definition: Choice of dressing matched to wound exudate, infection status and tissue type, e.g. alginate, foam, hydrocolloid or silver.
terms.exampleSentence: Wound dressing selection was changed to a silver foam due to clinical signs of local infection.
terms.contextNotes: Match dressing to wound, not the other way round.

terms.definition: Structured transfer of care including risk status, treatment plan and review interval to community podiatry services.
terms.exampleSentence: Handover to community podiatry included her high-risk status and 6-weekly review interval.
terms.contextNotes: Use SBAR or equivalent structured handover format.

terms.definition: Instructions and dressing regimen following nail surgery or debridement, including bathing, dressing changes and red-flag symptoms.
terms.exampleSentence: Post-operative wound care advice included daily saline soaks and dressing change for 14 days.
terms.contextNotes: Provide written instructions and emergency contact details.
